---
title: "Still no NVIDIA at more than 640 x 480"
date: 2007-04-13
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by censored77 on 2007-04-13
I've posted a couple of times and tried a few things. I just ran Envy and it all seemed to go ok, but my machine simply will not give me anything more than 640 x 480.

In xorg.0.log I get the error *No valid modes for "1280x1024"; removing.* for every resolution other than 640 x 480.

Can someone please, please help?!

xorg.conf :

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Feb 26 23:38:46 PST 2007

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

	# path to defoma fonts

    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"

EndSection

    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"

    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

EndSection

Section "Screen"

    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "UseFBDev" "true"
    SubSection     "Display"

### Post by xopher on 2007-04-13
> Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Generic Monitor"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 79.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Are you sure these values are correct? As they could cause the problem you're experiencing..

Check the values using this command:

```
sudo ddcprobe | grep monitorrange
```

More information regarding this: [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/FixVideoResolutionHowto](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/FixVideoResolutionHowto)

At the bottom of the page there's a section about the nvidia driver.

---

### Post by censored77 on 2007-04-13
They weren't quite right, but fixing them hasn't helped...

---

### Post by DoubleQuadword on 2007-04-13
Did you read the guide xopher suggested?

Anyway, if not try reconfiguring your **xorg.conf** file:

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

Eventually you'll be able to select which resolution/s you want along with your monitor's Horizontal Sync and Vertical Refresh. You should set up those values according to what your monitor can actually support though.

Also, don't expect a driver installation to fix this issue because drivers are not *closely* related to this matter since you can perform this configuration without installing them previously.

Regards.

---

### Post by censored77 on 2007-04-13
Yes, I've done that several times and tried the things in the howto guide. The driver is working and the resolution settings in xorg.conf are all there - it  just fails to load them. Could it be a monitor issue rather than the graphics drivers?

---

How big is your monitor and what brand/model?

### Post by censored77 on 2007-04-13
OK, the relevant bit of xorg.0.log seems to be related to the monitor:

```
(--) NVIDIA(0): Connected display device(s) on GeForce FX 5200 at PCI:1:0:0:
(--) NVIDIA(0):     NUL (CRT-0)
(--) NVIDIA(0): NUL (CRT-0): 350.0 MHz maximum pixel clock

```

I've generated a modeline with
```
gtf 1280 1024 60
```

and pasted that into xorg.conf to give me
```
Section "Device"

Section "Monitor"

### Post by Medieval_Creations on 2007-04-13
I may have missed it skimming through the thread *(if so, I appologize)* but what version of the nvidia drivers are you using?  (nvidia-glx or nvidia-glx-legacy)

I have a GeForce 6200 and the nvidia-glx driver worked perfectly only ***after*** running "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg", which I know you've already tried.
```
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```
The 5200 may be supported in the nvidia-glx-legacy driver.

Might be worth a try installing the legacy driver and reconfiguring X.
```
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx-legacy
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by censored77 on 2007-04-13
It's the normal non-legacy. In fact, I'm now on the latest version from the nvidia site and installed with Envy.

Seems to be ok, as the nvidia settings program works ok, which is why I'm beginning to think it's a monitor setting?

---

### Post by snisd on 2007-04-19
I had the same issue.

For me it helped to tell the nvidia driver to ignore the devices EDID. Try to add this to the Device section:
```

    Option "UseEDID" "FALSE" 
```
My Section now looks like this:
```

    Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP"
    Option "NoBandWidthTest" "true"
    Option "NoPowerConnectorCheck" "true"
    Option "UseEDID" "FALSE
```

---

### Post by agkbill on 2007-05-16
:lolflag: 

It works!

I got it all to work by adding

Option "ModeValidation" "AllowNon60HzDFPModes, NoVertRefreshCheck, NoEdidMaxPClkCheck, NoHorizSyncCheck"

Under the device section in the xorg.conf file

---

### Post by paszczak on 2007-07-11
snisd Thank YOU VERY MUCH!
Your options worked for me! Great :-)

---
